category_title: Female students who use silence as a mode of active engagement
scenario_title: Group presentation on domestic violence with discursive inconsistencies
minimal_context: Elia is leading her group's presentation on domestic violence, sharing their critique of the problem and suggestions for remediation.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Elia, it's time for your group's presentation. Please share your critique of the problem of domestic violence and your suggestions for its remediation.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Speaking forcefully) I don’t understand how this can happen, how you can have such a big problem with domestic violence in today’s society!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What else did your group discuss about the conditions that lead to this?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Personally, I want to be a homemaker, but that’s because I want my kids to turn out o.k., and someone has to be home with them. If you’re both working, it’s just like ....

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Can you finish your thought? How does that connect to your critique of the problem?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Trails off) But … that’s the way it is.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Elia, it's time for your group's presentation. Please share your critique of the problem of domestic violence and your suggestions for its remediation.

STUDENT: (Speaking forcefully) I don’t understand how this can happen, how you can have such a big problem with domestic violence in today’s society!

TUTOR: What else did your group discuss about the conditions that lead to this?

STUDENT: Personally, I want to be a homemaker, but that’s because I want my kids to turn out o.k., and someone has to be home with them. If you’re both working, it’s just like ....

TUTOR: Can you finish your thought? How does that connect to your critique of the problem?

STUDENT: (Trails off) But … that’s the way it is.

task.content: Presenting a critique of the problem (domestic violence) and suggestions for its remediation

context.environment: Daily class discussions and group project presentations
context.social_setting: Leading a project group on Domestic Violence

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When presumptively ‘appropriate’ conversational topics and critiques incurred dissonance with the intimacies of students’ personal and familial lives or cultural mores
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Said forcefully, ‘I don’t understand how this can happen...’ then trailed off: ‘But … that’s the way it is.’
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Navigating apparent contradictions to maintain allegiance to their families and culture(s) while also moving through processes of conscientización; painfully recognized the fundamental conflicts that arose in her own testimonials
related_quotes: I don’t understand how this can happen, how you can have such a big problem [with domestic violence in today’s society]!
But … that’s the way it is.
Personally, I want to be a homemaker, but that’s because I want my kids to turn out o.k., and someone has to be home with them. If you’re both working, it’s just like ....

behavior_patterns.condition: During daily class discussions and in her group’s project presentations
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Sometimes a forceful voice for awareness and criticism of the conditions that lead to domestic violence and at other times the most immediate subverter of her own critiques
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Experiencing fundamental conflicts in her own testimonials and critiques
